title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Automation and Data Client Systems · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A research data operations team. The team needs production python automation defense with protocol-correct behavior, bounded authority, changed-case tests, and an operational defense. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Python Automation Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test concurrency, fairness, quotas, backpressure, retry amplification, and graceful overload under changed demand. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Defend protocol semantics, runtime behavior, security, reliability, testing, accessibility, operations, and changed-load evidence as one system. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Gate runtime patches, dependencies, tests, static analysis, vulnerability reachability, provenance, configuration, and rollback. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.instruction:
project case — Python stakeholder client release P-3 doubles traffic and body size while p95 latency, memory, retries, and queue depth rise.
